title: Inside the Taliban’s New Military Formation on the Durand Line
gist: Pakistan has shifted from reactive defense to a proactive cross-border campaign against militant networks in Afghanistan amid a worsening security environment driven by the TTP, the Balochistan Liberation Army, and ISKP. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

insights.claim: Pakistan has shifted from reactive defense toward a proactive campaign targeting not just militants but the broader infrastructure supporting them inside Afghan territory, with airstrikes in Nangarhar, Khost, and Paktika in February 2026 and precision strikes in Khost, Kunar, and Paktika in June 2026.
insights.speaker: Sarah Adams
insights.so_what: Escalating Pakistani strikes inside Afghanistan raise the risk of open Pakistan-Taliban conflict along the Durand Line — a regional stability and supply-route risk.
insights.quote: a shift from reactive defense toward a more proactive campaign against militant networks operating along the Afghanistan-Pakistan frontier

insights.claim: Pakistan faces a worsening multi-front security environment as the TTP continues attacks across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and the former tribal areas while the Balochistan Liberation Army has scaled up coordinated operations including its Herof 2.0 campaign.
insights.speaker: Sarah Adams
insights.so_what: Compounding TTP and BLA insurgencies strain Pakistan's security capacity — a backdrop for sovereign and CPEC-related risk.
insights.quote: The Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA) has demonstrated an increasing ability to coordinate large-scale operations in Balochistan, including its Herof 2.0 campaign.

insights.claim: Islamic State Khorasan Province (ISKP) remains active on both sides of the Afghanistan-Pakistan border, presenting a separate but overlapping challenge to Pakistani security forces.
insights.speaker: Sarah Adams
insights.so_what: A persistent ISKP threat complicates any Pakistan-Taliban accommodation and keeps regional terrorism risk elevated.
insights.quote: Islamic State Khorasan Province (ISKP) remains active on both sides of the border, presenting a separate but overlapping challenge.
